# ZEDAS Project

> Mapping the future of water-based production. ZEDAS (Water Availability and
> Water-Resilient Economic Zones) is an international initiative of the Cámara
> Argentina del Agua (CAA) that combines hydrology, economics, industrial policy,
> and geospatial intelligence to identify territories where water can support
> long-term economic development. Thesis: production follows water.

## Pilot

- 16 pilot countries across Latin America, Africa, and Asia (4 continents)
- 7 core water & economic variables; 6 assessment dimensions; 1 global methodology
- 100+ experts and institutions

## Key pages

- [Home](/): overview, the pilot, methodology, and the global map.
- [Global Map](/map): interactive pilot map classifying the 16 countries by their
  water profile (renewable resources, water stress, withdrawal, economic
  productivity) with per-country detail and side-by-side comparison.

## Notes

- This is an illustrative pilot release. All figures are a pilot extract from
  FAO AQUASTAT, World Bank, IMF, and the Water Footprint Network.
- The pilot phase is supported by the Danida Fellows Networkers Grant 2026
  (Danida Fellowship Centre, Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark).
